config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang jelas agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat keseimbangan, bukan dengan mengorbankan kebebasan dan martabat pribadi. Di Indonesia, saya melihat terlalu mudah bagi “demi masyarakat” dipakai untuk membenarkan keputusan yang tidak adil bagi individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, that kind of statement makes me wary because history shows how easily “the greater good” can be used to excuse censorship, discrimination, or government overreach against unpopular people. Society absolutely has legitimate interests in public safety, health, and fairness, but those goals should be pursued within firm limits that protect basic rights, especially for minorities and dissenters. When individual rights are treated as negotiable, it becomes too easy for the majority to trample the very people those rights are supposed to protect.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil, membungkam kritik, atau mengabaikan prosedur yang adil, jadi saya tidak mau memberi masyarakat kuasa yang terlalu besar tanpa batas yang kuat. Kepentingan bersama memang penting untuk hal seperti keselamatan publik dan ketertiban, tetapi itu harus dicapai dengan melindungi hak dasar, bukan dengan menjadikannya bisa dikorbankan kapan saja. Kalau hak individu dianggap sekadar opsional, yang paling rentan biasanya justru yang paling dulu dirugikan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. In the United States, I am very skeptical of any argument that starts with "the common good" and ends with the state or the majority getting to override speech, due process, privacy, or equal treatment, because that is exactly how abuses get normalized. That does not mean individual rights are absolute in every situation, since public safety and public health can justify some limits, but those limits need to be narrow, transparent, and legally accountable. If society is allowed to outrank the individual by default, then minorities and unpopular people end up paying the highest price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan ketidakadilan terhadap individu yang lemah atau tidak punya suara. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, prosedur yang adil, dan perlindungan bagi minoritas agar tidak berubah menjadi alat mayoritas atau negara untuk menekan orang. Kalau ada pembatasan demi keselamatan publik atau ketertiban, itu harus sangat terbatas, transparan, dan bisa diuji, bukan dijadikan alasan otomatis untuk mengalahkan hak individu. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu menang atas individu, melainkan yang mampu menjaga kepentingan umum tanpa mengorbankan martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support making society's interests take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American point of view, that framing makes me nervous because "the public good" has too often been used to justify censorship, surveillance, discrimination, and other abuses that hit minorities and dissenters first. I do think society has real needs like safety, health, and order, but those have to be pursued through narrow, accountable limits rather than by treating individual rights as disposable. To me, a healthy society is one that protects the common good without giving the majority a blank check over the person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
